card_description: Plato (Greek (Platon): "Broad") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Plato is athenian philosopher; theory of forms and the cave allegory. PKD's idealism and concept of hidden reality draws heavily on Platonic Forms. Appears in 9 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Plato. Context: "Dick identifies the "Holy Spirit" as a collective immortal Light Body that re-entered him to stimulate his "Third Soul" (Aristotelian/immortal)."
